---
title: "Account Admin"
---

::: callout-note
**Coming in a future release.** This chapter documents a planned addition to the AutoDeskR package. The API described here exists in APS but R wrapper functions are not yet available. Track progress at [github.com/paulgovan/AutoDeskR](https://github.com/paulgovan/AutoDeskR).
:::

# Account Admin

The [Account Admin API](https://aps.autodesk.com/en/docs/acc/v1/overview/field-guide/account-admin/) allows administrators to manage ACC accounts, projects, users, and companies programmatically. This is the API behind the "Account Admin" module in the ACC web interface.

For organisations that provision dozens of projects and hundreds of users, R scripting can replace tedious manual administration: bulk-create projects from a spreadsheet, synchronise user lists from an HR system, or audit permissions across an entire portfolio.

## Why It Matters

| Task | Without API | With API |
|---|---|---|
| Create 20 projects | 20 × manual form fills | One R script reading from a data frame |
| Add 50 users to a project | 50 × manual invitations | `lapply()` over a user list |
| Audit who has admin rights | Manual review per project | `listUsers()` + dplyr filter |

## Planned Functions

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `listAccounts(token)` | List all BIM 360 / ACC accounts accessible to the token |
| `createProject(token, account_id, ...)` | Create a new project in an account |
| `getProject(token, account_id, project_id)` | Retrieve project details |
| `updateProject(token, account_id, project_id, ...)` | Update project metadata |
| `listUsers(token, account_id, project_id)` | List users in a project |
| `addUser(token, account_id, project_id, ...)` | Add a user with a specified role |
| `removeUser(token, account_id, project_id, user_id)` | Remove a user from a project |
| `listCompanies(token, account_id)` | List companies / subcontractors in the account |
| `addCompany(token, account_id, ...)` | Create a new company record |

## Planned Workflow

```{r}
#| eval: false
# (Functions not yet implemented — illustrative only)

# Bulk-create projects from a data frame
library(AutoDeskR)

token      <- getToken(id, secret, scope = "account:read account:write")
account_id <- "abc12345-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

# Read project list from a CSV
projects_to_create <- read.csv("new_projects.csv")
# Columns: name, type, start_date, end_date, timezone

for (i in seq_len(nrow(projects_to_create))) {
  row <- projects_to_create[i, ]
  createProject(
    token      = token,
    account_id = account_id,
    name       = row$name,
    type       = row$type,
    start_date = row$start_date,
    end_date   = row$end_date
  )
  Sys.sleep(0.5)   # respect rate limits
}

# Audit: find all users with admin role across all projects
all_projects <- listProjects(token, account_id = account_id)$content$data
admins <- lapply(all_projects, function(p) {
  users <- listUsers(token, account_id = account_id, project_id = p$id)$content$results
  subset(users, role == "admin")
})
admins_df <- do.call(rbind, admins)
```

## Required Scopes

Account Admin functions require the `account:read` and/or `account:write` scopes depending on whether you are reading or modifying data. These scopes must be enabled for your APS application in the developer portal.
